steps.title: Recognize the square terms
steps.content: The expression inside the parentheses is $x^2-144y^2$. Since $144y^2=(12y)^2$, this is a difference of two squares.

steps.title: Apply the factoring pattern
steps.content:
A difference of squares factors using the pattern $a^2-b^2=(a-b)(a+b)$. Here, $a=x$ and $b=12y$, so:
$$x^2-144y^2=x^2-(12y)^2=(x-12y)(x+12y)$$

steps.title: Keep the outside factor
steps.content:
The factor $19$ multiplies the whole factored expression:
$$19(x^2-144y^2)=19(x-12y)(x+12y)$$
The answer is D.
